overview: Blue Cloudpost is a Legacy tempo deck that leverages Kozilek's Command for flexible card advantage and disruption, while using Karn, the Great Creator to control the board through artifact conversion and untap effects. Thundertrap Trainer serves as a scalable threat that grows into a finisher or board-clearing engine through its counter and damage mechanics. Success relies on sequencing these signature pieces to outmaneuver Legacy's fast-paced threats while maintaining resource parity.
win_conditions: Winning with Thundertrap Trainer after repeated +1/+1 counter applications
Overwhelming the opponent with Karn's artifact conversion and untap abilities
Closing out games with Kozilek's Command damage mode after stripping opponent's resources

keep_criteria: Hands with Kozilek's Command for early disruption
Hands containing Karn, the Great Creator with a play-around plan
Hands with Thundertrap Trainer and cheap interaction
Keep hands with 2+ mana sources (Cloudpost, Darksteel Citadel, Moxen, or fetches).
Keep hands with at least one Turn 1 play and a Turn 2 spell (Kozilek's Command, Karn, or a threat).
Hands with 1 land and 2+ Moxen are keepers.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking instant-speed interaction in a Legacy environment
Hands with only high-cost permanents and no draw
Hands missing a clear path to stabilize against aggressive Legacy archetypes
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no early mana, or only late-game spells with no acceleration.

early_game: Prioritize playing Kozilek's Command to draw into answers or disrupt opponent's turns
Deploy Thundertrap Trainer to establish board presence and generate incremental value
Hold up mana for interaction while assessing opponent's opening strategy
Turn 1: Play a Mox or Cloudpost/Darksteel Citadel.
If you have two mana rocks, play them.
Turn 2: If you have 3+ mana, cast Kozilek's Command or Karn.
Sequence Cloudpost carefully—it enters tapped, so you need another mana source to cast spells the turn it hits.
Darksteel Citadel is crucial for hexproof and consistent mana.
mid_game: Use Karn, the Great Creator to convert opponent's threats into artifacts and untap them for control
Apply +1/+1 counters to Thundertrap Trainer to grow it into a board wipe or finisher
Cycle Kozilek's Command modes to maintain card advantage and manage tempo
Mana is your biggest asset but also your vulnerability.
Use Moxen strategically to enable spells when you need them.
Keep Cloudpost in play for late-game mana, but be aware of land destruction.
Manage hand size—Kozilek's Command can draw cards, so use it to refill when you're low.
Commit threats only when you have protection or can answer their interaction.
late_game: Leverage Karn's untap ability to repeatedly trigger artifact synergies and outvalue opponents
Push damage with a large Thundertrap Trainer while protecting it with Kozilek's Command
Transition to a grindy control game by stripping opponent's resources and closing with direct damage
Kozilek's Command (destroy all nonbasic lands, draw cards, or deal damage), Karn's ultimate (exile target permanent, then draw cards), or Thundertrap Trainer.
Usually, it's a combination of card advantage and a game-ending spell.
When you have enough mana to cast your win condition and have answered or ignored their interaction.
When they're at low life and you can close the game.
When you've established board control and they have no answers.

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.role: Primary source of card advantage and disruption
key_cards.notes: Use the draw mode early to find answers, switch to damage mode to close games, and utilize the untap mode to accelerate Karn's abilities.

key_cards.name: Karn, the Great Creator
key_cards.role: Board control and artifact synergy engine
key_cards.notes: Convert opponent's creatures to artifacts to enable untap control, and use his +1/+1 counter ability to protect himself or boost other permanents.

key_cards.name: Thundertrap Trainer
key_cards.role: Incremental advantage creature and potential finisher
key_cards.notes: Tap to add +1/+1 counters and flying for evasion, or untap to deal damage and generate card advantage through its triggered abilities.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Kozilek's Command before committing Karn to the battlefield to protect against early removal
Prioritize giving Thundertrap Trainer flying before attacking to avoid being blocked by aggressive threats
Use Karn's untap ability on your opponent's turn to disrupt their resource generation while maintaining board presence
Cloudpost enters tapped, so sequence carefully.
Darksteel Citadel is your best land for consistency and hexproof.
Kozilek's Command is incredibly flexible—use the right mode for the situation.
Thundertrap Trainer likely benefits from your mana base—use it to pressure or combo.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Kozilek's Command for early disruption and use Thundertrap Trainer's flying to bypass early blockers while growing counters.
Use Karn to convert their key counterspells or permanents into artifacts, forcing them to waste removal on untapped or converted pieces.
Disrupt their casting sequence with Kozilek's Command and use Karn's artifact conversion to break up their combo pieces if they rely on non-artifact permanents.

Use Kozilek's Command's split modes or Karn's abilities to bypass counters. Cast spells in response to their counters, or use mana rocks to cast multiple spells in a turn. Sometimes you can't beat counters, so focus on out-value and protecting your win conditions.
Out-ramp them with Moxen and Cloudpost. Use Kozilek's Command to destroy their mana rocks or nonbasics. Karn can exile their key ramp pieces. Force them to use resources on you while you set up.

It's a race for card advantage and protection. Prioritize protecting your Cloudpost/Citadel. Use Kozilek's Command to destroy their nonbasics. Sideboard in more hate or counterspells. Win by out-resourcing and forcing them to make mistakes.

common_threats: Watch for land destruction (Wasteland, Field of Ruin), artifact hate (Rest in Peace, Null Rod), and combo win conditions.
Pay attention to when they tap out for interaction vs. when they're holding up counters.
Track their graveyard for Sneak & Show or Delver strategies.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: signature cards and Legacy format pace
